UAReferenceType.Description: Links a BoundItem to the companion-specification Variable or Method in the AddressSpace that it exposes for a scenario. The target is the authoritative semantic node; the BoundItem does not copy its meaning.

Links a ScenarioBinding to the optional OPC UA Part 14 PubSub node(s) that realize it (a PublishedDataSet, DataSetWriter, DataSetReader or an ActionTarget). Forward 'ScenarioRealizedBy' reads binding -> realization; the inverse 'RealizesScenario' reads realization -> binding. Absent (and never required) when the binding is not realized over PubSub - a Server may instead serve the binding over the classic client/server (RPC) interface.

Links a ScenarioProfile in the Scenarios registry to a ScenarioBinding that serves that scenario, so a Client can start at the scenario it cares about and browse straight to every binding serving it (across companion specifications). Forward 'HasScenarioBinding' reads scenario -> binding; the inverse 'ServesScenario' reads binding -> scenario. The binding still lives physically under its per-specification ScenarioBindingGroup; this reference is the scenario-first discovery cross-link.

Links a derived or composing ScenarioBinding to a base ScenarioBinding whose fields it extends or composes (e.g. a Machine binding to the Device-facet binding it builds on). Optional browse convenience used where the base binding node is present in the same AddressSpace; the portable, cross-specification lineage carrier is ScenarioBinding.BaseDataSetClassIds.

UADataType.Description: The role the server offers for a ScenarioBinding, and hence what a client sets up on the other side.

Field.Name: Publisher
Field.Description: The server publishes the bound data; a client sets up a subscriber.

Field.Name: Subscriber
Field.Description: The server subscribes to the bound data; a client sets up a publisher.

Field.Name: ActionInvoker
Field.Description: The server invokes bound Methods/Actions on receipt; a client sends the trigger.

Field.Name: ActionResponder
Field.Description: The server responds to bound Actions; a client invokes them.

Field.Name: Bidirectional
Field.Description: Both data and action directions apply.

UADataType.Description: Generic role of a bound item for routing/bridging. It is intentionally domain-agnostic: a bridge maps each Kind to its target system without understanding the companion-specification semantics.

Field.Name: Telemetry
Field.Description: A measured/process value that changes continuously (maps to a time series).

Field.Name: Status
Field.Description: A discrete state/health/mode value.

Field.Name: Configuration
Field.Description: A configuration/parameter value that changes rarely.

Field.Name: Metric
Field.Description: An aggregated KPI or computed value.

Field.Name: Counter
Field.Description: A monotonically increasing counter/total.

Field.Name: Event
Field.Description: An event or condition (maps to a log/alarm stream).

Field.Name: Command
Field.Description: A bound Method/Action invocation (maps to an action).

Field.Name: Setpoint
Field.Description: A writable setpoint/target value.

Field.Name: Identification
Field.Description: Static nameplate/identity information.

Field.Name: Other
Field.Description: Any other role.

UADataType.Description: Whether a scenario binding realizes as a Part 14 data DataSet (PublishedDataItems) or an event DataSet (PublishedEvents). A binding is exactly one DataSet.

Field.Name: DataItems
Field.Description: A data DataSet: grouped Variable values (PublishedDataItemsType).

Field.Name: Events
Field.Description: An event DataSet: selected event fields from a notifier (PublishedEventsType).

UADataType.Description: Machine-readable descriptor of a single bound item: how to LOCATE it (BrowsePath relative to StartingNode, or an absolute SourceNodeId), its routing role (Kind) and the SEMANTIC cross-reference back to the companion model (TypeDefinition, BrowseName, ModelNamespaceUri, SemanticReferenceUri), which is retained so it can be exported to a disconnected consumer.

Field.Name: FieldName
Field.Description: Stable logical field name; matches the PubSub DataSet field name.

Field.Name: Kind
Field.Description: Generic routing role of the item.

Field.Name: AttributeId
Field.Description: Attribute of the source node to expose (default 13 = Value).

Field.Name: SamplingIntervalHint
Field.Description: Recommended sampling/publishing interval, in ms.

Field.Name: IndexRange
Field.Description: Optional sub-range for array values.

Field.Name: StartingNode
Field.Description: Node the BrowsePath is resolved from (default: the bound root).

Field.Name: BrowsePath
Field.Description: RECOMMENDED locator: RelativePath from StartingNode (type-level, portable).

Field.Name: SourceNodeId
Field.Description: Alternative absolute locator (instance/server-specific).

Field.Name: OwningObjectPath
Field.Description: For a bound Method: RelativePath to the Object it is called on (default: the bound root).

Field.Name: SourceTypeDefinition
Field.Description: TypeDefinition of the source node (semantic identity).

Field.Name: SourceBrowseName
Field.Description: Namespace-qualified BrowseName of the source node.

Field.Name: ModelNamespaceUri
Field.Description: Namespace URI of the companion model that defines the source.

Field.Name: DataSetFieldId
Field.Description: GUID correlating this item to Part 14 FieldMetaData.dataSetFieldId.

Field.Name: SourceScenarioBindingClassId
Field.Description: Provenance: DataSetClassId of the base scenario binding this field originates from (its facet). Lets a subscriber partition a composed DataSet into exact per-base-class field subsets. Absent for fields defined by this binding itself.

Field.Name: SemanticReferenceUri
Field.Description: Optional external semantic identifier (e.g. IRDI/CDD) for the item.

Field.Name: EventFieldOperand
Field.Description: For an event-DataSet field: the Part 14 SimpleAttributeOperand that selects it (alternative/complement to BrowsePath, whose segments are then relative to the event TypeDefinition).

UAObjectType.Description: A single item bound into a scenario: it references the companion-spec node it exposes (BindsToNode and/or a BrowsePath) and carries the routing role (Kind) and the semantic cross-reference retained for export.

UAVariable.Description: Stable logical field name of the item.
Generic routing role of the item.
Attribute of the source node to expose (default 13 = Value).
RECOMMENDED locator: RelativePath from the bound root; resolved per instance.
Node the BrowsePath is resolved from (default: the bound root).
Alternative absolute locator (instance/server-specific).
Recommended sampling/publishing interval (ms).
Optional sub-range for array values.
TypeDefinition of the source node (semantic identity).
Namespace-qualified BrowseName of the source node.
Namespace URI of the companion model defining the source.
GUID correlating the item to Part 14 FieldMetaData.

Provenance: DataSetClassId of the base scenario binding this field originates from (its facet). Lets a subscriber partition a composed DataSet into exact per-base-class field subsets. Absent for fields defined by this binding itself.
Optional external semantic identifier (e.g. IRDI/CDD).

UAObjectType.Description: A bound Variable exposed as a PubSub DataSet field.
A bound Method exposed as an invokable action; may be realized as a Part 14 Action/ActionTarget.

UAVariable.Description: RelativePath to the Object the Method is called on (default: the bound root).

UAObjectType.Description: A bound event field of an event DataSet, selected by a Part 14 SimpleAttributeOperand. Its BrowsePath is resolved relative to the event TypeDefinition (SourceTypeDefinition), not the AddressSpace instance; the EventSourcePath on the ScenarioBinding names the notifier it is selected from.

UAVariable.Description: The Part 14 SimpleAttributeOperand that selects this field (TypeDefinitionId, BrowsePath, AttributeId); maps directly to a PublishedEvents SelectedFields entry.

UAObjectType.Description: One scenario binding on a bound object or type. It declares the scenario URI and direction, lists the bound items (browsable and/or as a compact array), and may reference the Part 14 nodes that realize it.

UAVariable.Description: URI of the integration scenario this binding serves.
Role the server offers for this binding.
Version of the binding, aligned with the realizing DataSetMetaData.

Stable DataSetClassId (Part 14) identifying the class of the DataSet this binding defines, so subscribers recognize the same DataSet class across servers. It is a semantic class identity, not a guarantee of a fixed field layout (see the DataSetClassId clause). Deterministic.

DataSetClassIds of the base facet bindings this binding extends or composes (its class lineage). This binding's own DataSetClassId identifies the composed/derived class; a subscriber that knows a base class-id consumes the matching field subset (see BoundItemType.SourceScenarioBindingClassId).
Whether the binding realizes as a data DataSet (PublishedDataItems) or an event DataSet (PublishedEvents).

RelativePath to the cardinality level: the Server/bridge produces one DataSet per matched instance of it (default: the bound root); placeholders below it become fields. The DataSetClassId is shared across those DataSets (one class, many writers).
Part 14 DataSetMetaData for this DataSet (fields, dataSetClassId, configurationVersion), exposed so a consumer gets the class schema offline.
For an event DataSet: RelativePath to the event notifier to subscribe to (default: the cardinality anchor, i.e. the bound root when DataSetCardinalityPath is omitted).
For an event DataSet: optional ContentFilter (event where-clause).
Compact machine-readable list of bound items (the DataSet fields).

UAObject.Description: A browsable bound item (rich form of a BoundItems entry).

UAObjectType.Description: A per-companion-specification anchor grouping that spec's ScenarioBinding objects, so bindings from different companion specifications registered in one container never collide by BrowseName. Identified by CompanionSpecificationUri (a stable spec-level identifier, distinct from a namespace URI, because a companion specification may define several namespace URIs).

UAVariable.Description: Stable spec-level identifier of the companion specification this group anchors. Groups are unique per CompanionSpecificationUri.
All namespace URIs the companion specification defines/covers.

UAObject.Description: A scenario binding of this companion specification.

UAObjectType.Description: A discoverable container of per-companion-spec ScenarioBindingGroup objects, enumerated by Browse. A server exposes one server-wide instance under the Server object, and/or a local instance on any object that implements IScenarioBoundType.

UAObject.Description: A per-companion-specification group of scenario bindings.

UAObjectType.Description: A registered integration scenario: its URI plus human-readable metadata. The registry is extensible; vendors and other specifications add profiles with their own URIs.

UAVariable.Description: The scenario URI.
Short human-readable title.
Human-readable description of the scenario and its intended consumers.
Keywords describing the scenario.

UAObjectType.Description: Interface implemented by a companion-specification ObjectType (or instance) to advertise that it participates in scenario bindings, by exposing a local ScenarioBindings container.

UAObject.Description: The scenario bindings defined on this object.
Server-wide registry of scenario bindings, discoverable by browsing the Server object. Its presence does not require any PubSub configuration.
Real-time operational monitoring: SCADA/HMI, dashboards and observability platforms (e.g. OpenTelemetry). Low latency, cyclic telemetry and status.
Condition- and usage-based trending fed to maintenance analytics to forecast wear and schedule service.
High-resolution, correlated signals for baseline modelling and deviation/outlier detection.
Power, load, demand and energy signals for load management, peak shaving and grid-services coordination.
Condition and event streams for operators, CMMS/EAM and safety functions.
Multi-site supervision, contractual reporting and regulatory compliance.
